steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The dash introduces the clarification "it's little more than timid variations on what's already been." This shows the narrator is judging something at its core or essential level — not on the surface, not emotionally, not in terms of frequency. The blank must mean 'at the most essential level.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'superficially': means at the surface level, the opposite of what the dash-clause reveals. ✗
  • Choice B — 'ambivalently': means with mixed feelings, which has nothing to do with assessing essential nature. ✗
  • Choice C — 'fundamentally': means at the core or essential level, matching the narrator's judgment that the thing is merely timid variation at its very base. ✓
  • Choice D — 'frequently': means occurring often, which is unrelated to whether something is new in nature. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
